channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A sweeping power blackout leaves most of Chile in darkness
item.description: SANTIAGO, Chile (AP) — A sweeping blackout plunged most of Chile into darkness Tuesday, stranding commuters, knocking the internet offline and paralyzing businesses and daily life as officials scrambled to restore power.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE and MATIAS BASUALDO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Argentina's Milei skirts Congress to seat a divisive judge in the Supreme Court
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentina's President Javier Milei temporarily appointed two Supreme Court judges by decree Tuesday, bypassing Congress during its summer recess to push through a particularly controversial candidate in a move criticized as an overreach of executive power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least four dead in South Korea highway construction project collapse
item.description: At least four people were killed and six others were injured after concrete structures supporting a bridge collapsed at a highway construction site in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine and US have agreed on a framework economic deal, Ukrainian officials say
item.description: Three senior Ukrainian officials say that Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals.
item.dc:creator: Susie Blann And Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical but stable condition Tuesday as he worked from the hospital while battling double pneumonia, and the Vatican announced some major governing decisions that suggest he is getting essential work done and looking ahead.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NATO nation Poland scrambles air defenses as Russia strikes western Ukraine
item.description: Poland once again launched air defenses after Russia fired long-range strikes across Ukraine, hitting near the shared border with Poland just hours after leaders met in Kyiv to pledge more support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin appears to contradict Trump on Ukraine peacekeepers
item.description: The Kremlin appeared to contradict President Trump’s claim that Russia would accept peacekeeping troops in Ukraine. “There is a position on this matter that was expressed by Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov,” Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said, according to Politico.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: The Botswana government has signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Sello Motseta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin ready to work with US companies on mining rare-earth deposits in Russian-occupied Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said his nation was ready to work with U.S. companies to secure rare raw minerals within the nation’s borders in hopes of ending the conflict in Ukraine after three years of war.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medics say 6 babies have died from the cold in Gaza as displaced people shelter in tents and rubble
item.description: Palestinian medics say at least six infants have died from hypothermia in the last two weeks in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trapped in Gaza: Residents speak out as Egypt blocks exit, rejects Trump’s relocation plan
item.description: Gazans trapped by closed borders voice frustration, with some supporting Trump’s relocation plan as Egypt blocks exits and Hamas clings to control amid devastation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial begins in Germany for 4 alleged Hamas members accused of seeking weapons caches across Europe
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A trial began Tuesday in Germany for four alleged Hamas members suspected of organizing weapons caches across Europe. The country's top prosecutor accuses the men of membership in a foreign terrorist organization. It’s a pilot case for prosecutors, German news agency dpa reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Desperation grows in northern Gaza as Palestinians struggle to rebuild their homes
item.description: BEIT LAHIYA, Gaza Strip (AP) — When night falls over northern Gaza, much of the cityscape of collapsed buildings and piled wreckage turns pitch black.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED and ABDEL KAREEM HANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's national dialogue conference held as the battered country seeks to rebuild
item.description: Syria’s new rulers have held a long-awaited national dialogue conference to rebuild the country after nearly 14 years of civil war and the fall of former President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Omar Albam And Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Son of Chinese journalist jailed for espionage calls for his father's release
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Chinese journalist accused of espionage called for his father's release from a seven-year prison sentence in the high-profile case that signaled Beijing's tighten grip on journalism.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: Thousands of individuals have signed an online petition urging the revocation of tech billionaire Elon Musk's Canadian citizenship over his involvement in the Trump administration, which the petitioners argue undermines Canada's sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: A police union in Haiti demands more protection a day after gangs kill a Kenyan policeman
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Haitian police officers on Monday demanded better protection and treatment a day after gunmen killed a Kenyan policeman who was part of a U.N.-backed mission tasked with fighting gangs in the troubled Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More pressure on Rwanda as Congo says rebel uprising has killed over 7,000 people this year
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — More than 7,000 people have died this year as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have captured unprecedented amounts of territory in mineral-rich eastern Congo, Congo's prime minister said Monday, as the European Union announced it would review an agreement with Rwanda on critical raw m
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and WILSON MCMAKIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On third anniversary of Ukraine invasion, European leaders show support, express unease
item.description: European leaders voiced support for Ukraine on the third anniversary of the Russia invasion while demanding a say in any US-Russia peace arrangement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis resting on day 10 in hospital for lung infection that led to early stages of kidney insufficiency
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, was resting Monday morning, his 10th day at the hospital for a complex lung infection that led to the early stages of kidney insufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan's only women-led radio station to resume operations after Taliban lifts suspension
item.description: An Afghan radio station will resume broadcasts after the Taliban lifted a suspension imposed over alleged cooperation with a foreign country's TV channel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis remains in 'critical condition' with signs of early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis remains hospitalized in "critical condition" with pneumonia, but the Vatican says he rested well overnight.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation
